free_text: Differential cross sections for the B-11(p,a)Be-8 and
B-11(p,p)B-11 reactions, suitable for ion beam analysis
5.5 MV TN11 Tandem Accelerator of
N.C.S.R. 'Demokritos', Athens, Greece
A highly pure (>99.9%),isotopically
enriched, approx.99%, B-11 target deposited on a mirror
polished  thick tantalum backing was used for the
experiments. The target  had an active surface of
1*1 cm**2, and the nominal thickness was reported
as 323 mug/cm**22.
The detection system consisted of four Si
surface barrier detectors (Thickness: 1000 mum,
set at 10 ang. intervals) along with the
corresponding electronics.
.The total estimated error is reported by author
in tables-varied between 3-4% (pending on the detector)
The statistical error from peak integration also varied
between approx.1-5%.
.The error in target thickness.
.The error is reported by authors in tables.
SB
SD: SF5=PAR was added to REACTION code in
and E-LVL=0 was added to COMMON in Subent 3
.TABLE 1 of J,NIM/B,268,3539,2010
.The combined statistical errors,
varying between 4-5%, pending on the detector/angle
combination, included uncertainties in the product
Q*Omega and in peak integration, including counting
statistics and background subtraction.
.TABLE 2 of J,NIM/B,268,3539,2010
.The combined statistical errors,
varying between 5-7%, pending on the detector/angle
combination, included uncertainties in the product
Q*Omega and in peak integration, including counting
statistics and background subtraction.
SD: SF5=PAR was added to REACTION code in
and E-LVL=0 was added to COMMON.
